content: Just as the word 'reflexion' carries dual meanings of physical light reflection and deliberate cognitive reflection in human language learning, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to incorporate a dual 'light reflection' step: bouncing generated outputs off external human language usage distributions (like the curated vocabulary datasets from high school literacy curricula) to catch mismatches between the LLM's internal reasoning and common-sense human expressive norms, before doing a second round of internal self-critique. This hybrid approach leverages the dual identity of the concept 'reflexion' that bridges human pedagogy and AI agent design.
